candidates.why: Primary operating revenue line for a shipping company; consistent, material amounts across years and part of operating revenues total.
Secondary operating revenue stream; also clearly revenue and part of operating revenues and other income total.
Interest income is financial income, not operating revenue, so not appropriate for REVT.
Includes net income plus OCI items, not top-line revenue.
Balance sheet asset representing revenues earned but not yet billed/received, not the revenue flow itself.

variables.notes: Using both main operating revenue lines as a priority list; downstream logic should take the first non-missing per period.

candidates.why: Direct operating costs of running vessels; best representation of cost of sales for a shipping company.
Also direct voyage-related costs, but secondary to overall vessel operating expenses as a primary COGS proxy.
Overhead SG&A, not direct cost of revenues; should be excluded from COGS.
Financial result, not operating production/service cost.
Other non-core items; not direct cost of sales.
Financial income/expense, not COGS.

variables.notes: Selected the primary direct operating cost line as COGS; voyage expenses and commissions are also direct but treated as secondary to avoid multiple COGS rows.

candidates.why: This is income, not an expense; does not represent SG&A overhead.
Direct operating costs (COGS-equivalent), not overhead SG&A.
Classical SG&A overhead line; clearly fits XSGA definition.

variables.notes: Only the G&A line qualifies as SG&A; other candidates are either income or direct operating costs.
No R&D-related line appears in the Income Statement or Balance Sheet preview; for this shipping business R&D expense is likely not reported separately, so XRD is treated as 0.

candidates.why: Main reported interest expense line; best primary choice for XINT.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Capital Financing - Gross
candidates.why: More specific interest expense label that appears in some periods; appropriate fallback if main interest expense line is missing.
Capitalized interest is not an expense in the period’s P&L; should not be used as XINT itself.
Represents allocation of profit to minority interest, not interest expense on debt.

candidates.row_label: Unrealized gain (loss) on interest rate swaps
candidates.why: Fair value movement on derivatives, not interest expense.
Equity line, not an interest expense.
Equity line, not an interest expense.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Capital Financing - Gross

variables.notes: Provided a priority list of interest expense labels; capitalized interest is excluded because it is not a period expense in the income statement.

candidates.why: Represents equity attributable to shareholders; minority interests are reported separately, so this is the correct BE base.
Includes both liabilities and equity; not suitable as pure book equity.

variables.notes: Minority interests are reported on separate lines, so using Total shareholders equity avoids double counting when adding MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit, not the balance sheet minority equity amount needed for MIB.
Balance sheet line for equity attributable to non-controlling interests; best match for MIB.
Appears to duplicate Non-controlling interest with same values; selecting only one avoids double counting.
Income statement flow, not the stock of minority equity; inappropriate for MIB denominator.

variables.notes: Chose the modern label ‘Non-controlling interest’ as the single MIB line to avoid double counting with the duplicate ‘Minority Interest’ row.
